definition:
πτωχός, -ή, -όν
 (πτώσσω, to crouch, cower), [in LXX for עָנִי, דַּל, רָשׁ, etc. ;] 
of one who crouches and cowers, hence, 
   1. as subst., a beggar: Luk.14:13, 21 16:20, 22. 
   2. As adj., 
   (a) prop., beggarly: metaphorically, στοιχεῖα, Gal.4:9 (see Lft., in l); 
   (b) in broader sense (opposite to πλούσιος), poor: Mat.11:5 19:21 26:9, 11, Mrk.10:21 12:42-43 14:5, 7, Luk.4:18 7:22 18:22 19:8 21:3, Jhn.12:5-6, 8 13:29, Rom.15:26, 2Co.6:10, Gal.2:10, Jas.2:2-3, 6, Rev.13:16; π. τ. κόσμῳ, Jas.2:5; metaphorically, Luk.6:20, Rev.3:17; π. τ. πνεύματι, Mat.5:3.†
 SYN.: πένης, q.v  (AS)
